title: All endpoints served under /api prefix, not the spec's root paths
evidence: app.go:329-339 registers routes in an r.Group("/api") so the server exposes POST /api/books and GET /api/health, whereas TASK.md/REQUIREMENTS specify POST /books and GET /health; README.md:57-166 documents the /api paths, but app_test.go:44-49 registers root paths so production /api routing is never exercised.
suggestion: Register the routes at root (drop the /api group) to match the spec, or align the spec/tests with the /api prefix.

title: Production route wiring (main's /api group) is not covered by any test
evidence: Tests build their own routers at root paths (app_test.go:39-52, 67-68, 125-126); main()'s r.Group("/api") registration (app.go:329-340) is never invoked, consistent with stored test_coverage=0.545 (~45% uncovered).
suggestion: Add a test that boots the real router (extract route setup into a testable func) so the served paths are verified.

title: year and isbn required beyond the spec (only title, author required)
evidence: app.go:28-29 tags Year and ISBN with binding:"required"; Year being an int, required also rejects year 0. Spec R9 requires only title and author.
suggestion: Remove required from Year/ISBN so a book with only title+author (or year 0) is accepted.

title: Tests build the id path with string(rune(id+'0')), only correct for ids 1-9
evidence: app_test.go:240, 289, 339 use "/books/"+string(rune(id+'0')); works only because in-memory DBs yield id=1, breaks for id>=10.
suggestion: Use strconv.FormatInt(id, 10) to build the path.

title: Explicit empty title/author checks are unreachable after binding:required
evidence: app.go:79-91 and 239-251 re-check empty title/author, but ShouldBindJSON already rejects them via binding:"required" (app.go:27-28).
suggestion: Drop the redundant checks or drop the binding tags, not both.
